# MD Project Progress

## Session: 2025-12-14

### Completed
- [x] Project initialized with Tauri 2.0 + React + TypeScript
- [x] Ring UI integrated (@jetbrains/ring-ui-built for Vite compatibility)
- [x] Basic components created: App, Sidebar, FileList, PathBar, Terminal
- [x] Tauri plugins configured: shell, fs, opener
- [x] automation-mcp configured for visual QA testing

### In Progress
- [ ] Test app renders correctly after import fix (openPath)
- [x] Fix automation-mcp screenshot base64 encoding (fixed, needs MCP restart)

### Blocked
- None currently

### Known Issues
1. `openPath` import from '@tauri-apps/plugin-opener' - verify correct export name
2. Ring UI Table/List components may need specific import paths

### Next Session Tasks
1. Restart Claude to reload MCP with screenshot fix
2. Test MCP screenshot functionality
3. Debug and fix Ring UI import error
4. Test app renders correctly with Ring UI components
5. Verify terminal xterm.js integration works

### Git Status
- Last commit: Initial Tauri setup
- Uncommitted changes: Ring UI integration, component updates

---

## Notes for Future Sessions
- Always read this file first to understand current state
- Use features.json for detailed feature status
- Test via MCP automation, not automated test frameworks
- Ring UI requires @jetbrains/ring-ui-built (not @jetbrains/ring-ui) for Vite

[package]
name = "tauri-app"
version = "0.1.0"
description = "A Tauri App"
authors = ["you"]
edition = "2021"

# See more keys and their definitions at https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/reference/manifest.html

[lib]
# The `_lib` suffix may seem redundant but it is necessary
# to make the lib name unique and wouldn't conflict with the bin name.
# This seems to be only an issue on Windows, see https://github.com/rust-lang/cargo/issues/8519
name = "tauri_app_lib"
crate-type = ["staticlib", "cdylib", "rlib"]

[build-dependencies]
tauri-build = { version = "2", features = [] }

[dependencies]
tauri = { version = "2", features = [] }
tauri-plugin-opener = "2"
tauri-plugin-shell = "2"
tauri-plugin-fs = "2"
serde = { version = "1", features = ["derive"] }
serde_json = "1"

## JetBrains Ring UI Overview

Ring UI is a collection of JetBrains Web UI components.(no content)The latest version is 7.0.48, last published 4 days ago.

## Icon Usage in Ring UI

According to the Ring UI changelog, there have been significant changes to icon handling:

The ability to import SVG icons as components (import PencilIcon from '@jetbrains/icons/pencil.svg') deprecated earlier was removed. The Icon component now exports icons (@jetbrains/icons package) and logos (@jetbrains/logos) as React components.(no content)@jetbrains/icons was updated to 3.0.0 which includes a major rework of icons' shapes and dimensions. The size, width and height props are deprecated in the Icon component. The intrinsic size of the icon (width and height SVG attributes) is used instead.

### SVG Loader Configuration

For SVG handling, the changelog mentions this webpack loader configuration: `{ test: /\.svg$/, loader: require.resolve('svg-inline-loader'), options: {removeSVGTagAttrs: false}, include: [require('@jetbrains/icons')] }`(no content)SVG icons are not inlined anymore – Icon and IconNg components don't support sprite IDs. If you are patching svgSpriteLoader, replace "svgSpriteLoader.include.push(...)" with "svgInlineLoader.include.push(...)".

## Additional Resources

- CodeSandbox provides an online @jetbrains/ring-ui playground to view and fork example apps and templates.
- Ring UI aims to provide all the necessary building blocks for web-based products built inside JetBrains, as well as third-party plugins developed for JetBrains' products.

content.content:
# Button Component Props Interface

The `ButtonBaseProps` interface defines the component's properties. Here's how the icon prop works:

**Icon Prop Definition:**
```typescript
icon?: string | IconType | null | undefined;
```

The icon prop accepts three types:
- **string**: A string identifier for the icon
- **IconType**: A specific icon type from the icon system
- **null/undefined**: Optional, allowing buttons without icons

**Usage in Rendering:**
The icon is rendered as an `Icon` component when present. According to the code: "icon && (<Icon className={classNames(styles.icon, iconClassName)} glyph={icon} size={iconSize} suppressSizeWarning={iconSuppressSizeWarning} />)"

This means when an icon is provided, it's passed to the Icon component's `glyph` prop, which handles the actual icon rendering. The component also supports an `iconRight` prop for icons positioned on the right side of the button content.
